serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml aluminium can of cola, the typical single-serve portion widely available in Singapore convenience stores and supermarkets.

nutrient_source.title: Cola, carbonated beverage — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for canned cola / carbonated cola beverage. All per-100g macro and micronutrient values preserved from the input record which carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Soft drink, cola, regular — Glycemic Index Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 for regular cola (sugar-sweetened carbonated cola beverage) sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 63 × (10.36 × 3.30) / 100 = 63 × 34.19 / 100 ≈ 21.6 per 330 ml can.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9.94 g sugar per 100 ml; independently associated with elevated blood pressure via insulin-mediated sodium retention and weight gain
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid regular cola consumption; the high sugar load (9.94 g/100 ml) independently raises blood pressure risk regardless of the low sodium content (3 mg/100 ml), and the caffeine (~34 mg per 355 ml can) may cause acute blood pressure elevation — substitute with plain water, unsweetened sparkling water, or plain green tea.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Rapidly absorbed liquid sugar (9.94 g/100 ml) with zero dietary fibre, causing acute postprandial glucose spikes and worsening insulin resistance with habitual use
type2_diabetes.guidance: Persons with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid canned cola entirely; the 9.94 g of sugar per 100 ml in liquid form is absorbed faster than equivalent solid-food carbohydrates, producing sharp postprandial glucose excursions, and habitual consumption worsens insulin resistance — switch to diet or zero-sugar cola, plain sparkling water, or unsweetened beverages.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Ultra-processed sugar-sweetened beverage with high glycaemic load per serving (GL ≈ 21.6); habitual consumption linked to elevated triglycerides, weight gain, and increased CVD risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit or eliminate canned cola; habitual sugar-sweetened beverage intake is independently associated with elevated triglycerides, increased visceral adiposity, and higher CVD event risk — replace with water, unsweetened herbal teas, or low-sugar alternatives.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100 ml) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; phosphoric acid may contribute to bone demineralisation in patients on corticosteroids; no anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Persons with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid canned cola; the high sugar load promotes systemic inflammation relevant to RA disease activity, phosphoric acid may worsen bone demineralisation in those on corticosteroid therapy, and the beverage provides no anti-inflammatory nutrients — replace with green tea or plain water.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Canned cola is an ultra-processed, carbonated sugar-sweetened beverage sold in standard aluminium cans, most commonly in 330 ml or 355 ml sizes. It is produced by dissolving sucrose or high-fructose corn syrup in carbonated water together with caramel colour (E150d), phosphoric acid, natural cola flavourings, and caffeine. The resulting drink is dark brown, highly effervescent, and characterised by a sweet, slightly acidic taste with a faint bitter note from caffeine and phosphoric acid. Per 100 ml it provides approximately 42 kcal, 10.36 g of carbohydrate (of which 9.94 g is sugar), 0.25 g of fat, negligible protein, and only 3 mg of sodium. Dietary fibre is absent. Cola is one of the most widely consumed beverages globally and is ubiquitous in Singapore convenience stores, hawker centres, and fast-food outlets. Clinically, canned cola is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and carries high concern ratings for Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. The liquid form of sugar is absorbed faster than equivalent solid-food carbohydrates, producing acute postprandial glucose spikes. Habitual SSB consumption is independently associated with insulin resistance, elevated triglycerides, weight gain, and increased blood pressure. The glycaemic index of regular cola is approximately 63 (medium GI), but the glycaemic load per standard 330 ml can is approximately 21.6, placing it in the high-GL category per serving. Phosphoric acid content raises additional concern for bone mineral density in populations on corticosteroid therapy. Healthier substitutes include diet or zero-sugar cola, plain sparkling water, unsweetened green tea, or plain water.
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in a can of cola?
Is cola bad for blood pressure?
What is the glycaemic index of cola?
Can I drink cola if I have hypertension?
Is cola suitable for rheumatoid arthritis?
How many calories in a can of cola?
What are the ingredients in cola?

confidence_reason: All core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95. GI value sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for regular cola. Source match is exact for both nutrient and GI data.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo: cola is a carbohydrate-sugar beverage containing no protein by formulation. fat_g is 0.25 g per HPB Foodo (trace fat, not zero). saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by formulation; cola contains no saturated fat. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero: cola is a clear liquid beverage with no fibre. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero by formulation. magnesium_mg is reported as 0.0 in HPB Foodo (below detection threshold / confirmed zero). monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in HPB Foodo for this beverage. water_g set to -1: moisture content not separately reported in HPB Foodo entry. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo for this beverage entry. Cultural classification in the input as 'Chinese' cuisine has been revised to 'Global / Western' as cola is an American-origin commercial beverage; however, Singaporean multilingual naming rules have been applied given the SG region tag and the food's widespread consumption in Singapore. GL per serving calculated as GI (63) × carbohydrate per serving in grams (10.36 g/100 ml × 3.30 = 34.19 g) / 100 = 21.6.
